claim: Key distinction: agents have non-deterministic looping workflows controlled by LLM; workflows are deterministic step-A→B→C. Agents have goals + tools; workflows are sequential tasks.
actionable: Before building, ask: Does LLM decide what executes and how many times? If yes → agent. If sequential → workflow. Build accordingly; wrong classification wastes tokens.
source_title: Are You Building REAL AI Agents or Just Using LLMs?

claim: Workflows are better for constrained outputs (social posts to platforms); agents better for exploratory goals (tech stack recommendation via iterative questioning)
actionable: Use workflows when you want deterministic behavior across all paths (no unexpected repetition/skipping); use agents when you want reasoning and adaptive iteration toward a goal.
source_title: Are You Building REAL AI Agents or Just Using LLMs?

claim: Agents require tools to interact with environment (Google Docs, GitHub, APIs); without tools, non-deterministic chatbot is not an agent despite non-sequential conversation
actionable: Verify your agent has tools bound before calling it an agent; a chatbot with fancy prompt is not an agent without environment interaction capability.
source_title: Are You Building REAL AI Agents or Just Using LLMs?

claim: Long-term memory agent (n8n template) saves notes to Google Docs and fetches them; demonstrates agent pattern with tool invocation based on query (save vs retrieve decision)
actionable: Implement persistent memory agents by binding save/retrieve tools and letting LLM decide when to invoke; enables agent to maintain context across sessions.
source_title: Are You Building REAL AI Agents or Just Using LLMs?

claim: GitHub agents are highly agentic: deciding which files to analyze, how many times to invoke tools, reasoning about repo structure; compare to ChatGPT web search (minimal agentic behavior)
actionable: Judge agentic behavior by agent autonomy in tool selection and invocation count; more decisions = more agentic. GitHub repo analysis >> web search chatbot.
source_title: Are You Building REAL AI Agents or Just Using LLMs?

claim: Anthropic distinguishes agents from workflows: agents loop until resolution found (unknown step count); workflows chain LLM calls in known sequence. Both valid but different problem spaces.
actionable: Reference Anthropic's agent vs workflow definitions when designing systems; recognize both patterns have use cases and are not interchangeable.
source_title: Are You Building REAL AI Agents or Just Using LLMs?
